steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a long, gentle S-curve down through the middle of where the pear will sit — this is the axis. Most pears lean a little, so let your line lean too.

steps.title: Block the two bulbs (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a small circle near the top of the axis and a larger oval near the bottom. The pear is essentially these two shapes stacked — a small head and a wider belly.

steps.title: Connect the silhouette (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, draw the two outer curves that link the small top to the wide bottom. Notice how the curve tightens at the neck and loosens out around the belly — that change is what makes it read as a pear.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the outline
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the full silhouette in one continuous pass, riding over your pencil guide. Let the line breathe — pears are soft, not mechanical.

steps.title: Ink the stem (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add a short, slightly bent stem rising from the top. A second tiny curve where it meets the fruit suggests the dimple where it joins.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and lift away any pencil guides that pull attention from the ink. Leave anything that still feels like quiet construction texture.
